Image 16: Rated 1 out of 5 stars

## I used this site for a year
I used this site for a year. It was fairly good until I got warnings that my prompts didn't match guidelines. What? I input simple words. It's not my fault the AI generator is rigid and unbalanced. I got shadowbanned. I couldn't add my image for the daily theme. Nobody saw my images, and I got no more likes. I deleted my account. There are tons of other AI image generators that aren't frustrating and don't treat you like an offender for their faults.

Among reviewers who comment on Playground's responsiveness, 97% feel positive. Users say the support team is quick, helpful, and easy to reach, often providing real-time human assistance. They appreciate the collaborative approach and willingness to implement feedback. However, a few mention minor frustrations with app functionality and occasional delays.

“Customer service is prompt. When you book with [sensitive content hidden] she knows the software really well and she know exactly what you are talking about even if you do not know what you are asking.”

Amanda A. [...] 3-4(19)

5(171)

### What do users say about Playground?

Reviewers indicate Playground streamlines daycare management by centralizing operations, improving organization, and making administrative tasks more efficient. They appreciate its intuitive, user-friendly interface, robust communication tools for parents and staff, and the ability to handle payments, attendance, and documentation in one platform. Many highlight the responsive and helpful customer support, which they say eases onboarding and daily use.  
  
Some say there is a learning curve due to the platform’s depth, and they occasionally encounter limitations with features or payment processing speed. They note the team is receptive to feedback and regularly adds new features based on user suggestions.

results.content:
border:  important to keep in the loose fill  and to better define your playground. “Here’s a border that’s beyond repair. So what you’ll want to do is unsecure the spikes,  take out your border, then replace it with new. Once you’ve done that you take your spikes  and you hammer it back down to  secure your border in place.” Have a hammer, it’s easy to get new  borders by the piece, and some long spikes,  and that hammer: pretty simple to  secure the border back into place. Number Three on our Top  Six to Fix list is swings. Swings are probably the most used and  abused pieces of playground equipment. "Alright we've got ourselves a broken seat here. Here’s a bucket, you can see here it's  already starting to crack..." These seats are pretty beat;  this belt seat is falling apart. "We [...] # How to Repair Playground Equipment Damages | 6 DIY Playground Fixes
## Playgrounds
37 subscribers
18 likes

### Description
4983 views
Posted: 3 May 2023
#playgrounds #playgroundrepairs #fixplaygroundequipment

Is the playground equipment at our school or park damaged, broken, or worn down? These kinds of issues can potentially be safety hazards to children playing on the playground. 

While some damages are more major, requiring complete replacement, there are some DIY fixes that can be done to your playground equipment! Take a look at this video and one of our Certified Playground Safety Inspectors (CPSI) will tell you how to fix these safety issues yourself.

Fixing these common playground safety hazards yourself is a big time and money saver: [...] “Hi I’m Petros and I'm with Playgrounds.com. And around here, I'm known as  one of the find it, fix it guys!” Our certified playground safety inspector team  is all about finding and fixing trouble spots and  safety issues on your playground and equipment. Here’s the Top Six to Fix: Loose Fill safety surfacing materials  like rubber mulch or wood mulch are popular,  cost-effective surfacing options for playgrounds. However... “Loose fill in your play area,  especially in high traffic zones like under  your slides, needs to be raked back so you  have a minimum depth of nine inches.” So it’s a good idea to have a rake and  an extra supply or a few bags of wood or  rubber mulch on hand for spot repairs. Another Top Six is your border:  important to keep in the loose fill  and to better

results.title: Public Playground Safety Handbook
results.content: General Upkeep of Playgrounds (§4) There are no user modifications to the equipment, such as strings and ropes tied to equipment, swings looped over top rails, etc. Remove string or rope Correct other modification The entire playground is free from debris or litter such as tree branches, soda cans, bottles, glass, etc. Clean playground There are no missing trash receptacles. Replace trash receptacle Trash receptacles are not full. Empty trash INSPECTION BY: 43 Handbook for Public Playground Safety Routine Inspection and Maintenance Issues Broken equipment such as loose bolts, missing end caps, cracks, etc. Broken glass & other trash Cracks in plastics Loose anchoring Hazardous or dangerous debris Insect damage Problems with surfacing Displaced loose-fill surfacing (see Section 4.3) Holes, [...] . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . 18 4.2 Repairs . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . 18 4.3 Maintaining Loose-Fill Surfacing . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . 18 4.4 Recordkeeping . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . 19 5 Parts of the Playground . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . 20 5.1 Platforms, Guardrails and Protective Barriers . . . . . . . . . . [...] head injury than a fall onto a hard surface. However, some injuries from falls, including broken limbs, may occur no matter what playground surfacing material is used. The most widely used test method for evaluating the shock absorbing properties of a playground surfacing material is to drop an instrumented metal headform onto a sample of the material and record the acceleration/time pulse during the impact. Field and laboratory test methods are described in ASTM F1292 Standard Specification for Impact Attenuation of Surface Systems Under and Around Playground Equipment. Testing using the methods described in ASTM F1292 will provide a “critical height” rating of the surface. This height can be considered as an approximation of the fall height below which a life-threatening head injury
